body.title: Provenance service: stamp every memory write with verified source token
body.result: test fixes (db_url isolation, patch target for lazy import) + uu channel.py source_agent stamp; all provenance writes wired
body.description:
Every write to clan.memories, channel_messages, and adc.palace should carry the verified provenance token of the writing agent — not self-reported agent_id, but the rack-issued token from T-provenance-identity. Also track derivation chains: if memory B was summarized from memory A, store derived_from=[A.id].

Schema additions to clan.memories: source_token (varchar, FK-soft to registry), source_agent (varchar, denormalized for query speed), derived_from (text[], array of source memory ids). All three nullable for migration safety — existing rows get NULL, new writes must supply source_token.

**Affected files:** clan.memories schema (migration), devices/librarian/memory_writer.py, devices/igor/memory/cortex.py (store() method)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres schema changes via migration file; derived_from enables chain-of-custody queries
**Scope boundary:** IN — source_token + source_agent + derived_from on clan.memories and channel_messages; OUT — adc.palace attribution (follow-on), retroactive backfill of existing rows
**Completion criteria:** new memory writes include source_token; SELECT source_agent, count(*) FROM clan.memories GROUP BY source_agent returns meaningful breakdown; derived_from populated when Librarian summarizes or Igor derives a memory from another
